reason: The watchdog timeout pair now has a clean baseline export, exact current-build ntoskrnl string hits plus Ghidra fallback artifacts, a repo-side PoFx pseudocode lead, a successful reboot-verified boot trace, a host-side ETL registry review, a DcomLaunch attribution package that narrows the svchost lead to the service host group containing Power, and three Procmon-adjacent runtime attempts: a working boot-log review with adjacent Session Manager\Power hits, a shell-safe post-boot DcomLaunch/Power trigger that still returned no exact watchdog reads, and an S1-specific follow-up that disturbed guest ops without leaving usable in-guest artifacts or wake-history confirmation. That gives the candidate a credible cross-layer evidence base, but there is still no shipped app mapping, no validated non-default profile, and no direct live read of the exact watchdog values themselves.
